channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news from verified Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda International Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus in DRC and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, signaling heightened global health risk.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Attack Kills Four in Russia, Moscow Faces Largest Assault in Over a Year
item.description: A major Ukrainian drone attack killed at least four people across Russian regions, with Moscow experiencing its largest aerial assault in more than a year as the war intensifies.

item.title: North Korean Soccer Club Arrives in South Korea for Asian Champions League
item.description: Naegohyang FC became the first North Korean athletes to visit South Korea in eight years, arriving for an Asian Women's Champions League semi-final amid strained inter-Korean relations.

item.title: Senate Removes Funding for Trump White House Ballroom From Spending Package
item.description: A U.S. Senate official stripped security funding that could be used for President Trump's planned White House ballroom from a massive spending package, imperiling Republican efforts to use taxpayer money for the project.

item.title: South Korea Vows All Options to Prevent Samsung Strike
item.description: South Korea's prime minister said the government will pursue all options including emergency arbitration to avoid a labor strike at Samsung Electronics, the country's largest employer.

item.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After Record 11-Month Deployment Supporting Iran War and Venezuela Operation
item.description: The worlds largest aircraft carrier returned to Virginia after 326 days at sea, the longest deployment since Vietnam War, having supported combat operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuelas Maduro. The crew received the Presidential Unit Citation for outstanding performance in combat.

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared a public health emergency of international concern after over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from the Bundibugyo virus variant, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. Cases have spread from Congo to Ugandas capital Kampala.

item.title: Russia Launches Largest Drone Assault Since War Began, Flattens Kyiv Apartment Building
item.description: Russia fired over 1,560 drones and missiles across Ukraine in three days, killing at least 9 in Kyiv when a nine-story apartment building collapsed. The massive strikes came during Trumps Beijing summit and followed a brief ceasefire, with Ukraine saying air defenses intercepted over 93 percent of incoming targets.

item.title: Pentagon Cancels Europe Troop Deployment Amid Frustrations With NATO Over Iran War
item.description: The Pentagon withdrew thousands of soldiers from a planned deployment to Poland as Trump expressed anger over European allies refusal to support the war with Iran. The move signals deepening US frustration with NATO burden-sharing as the Iran conflict strains alliance relations.

item.title: UAE Explains Why It Walked Out of OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates stated its departure from OPEC was a strategic decision based on energy market realities, not political considerations.

item.title: UK Government to Scrap Planned Rise in Fuel Tax
item.description: The UK government plans to abandon a planned fuel tax increase amid economic pressures and public discontent.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: US Continues Operations in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Live coverage of ongoing US military operations in the Strait of Hormuz as the conflict with Iran continues.

item.title: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Countries After Trump Threats
item.description: Canada is strengthening defense cooperation with Nordic nations in the Arctic region following US pressure and policy shifts under the Trump administration.

item.title: Oil Inventory Stockpile Implications of Iran War and Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Analysis of global oil inventory levels and strategic petroleum reserves as the Iran conflict disrupts shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Legacy of Limbo in Armenia After Loss of Nagorno-Karabakh
item.description: Armenia faces an uncertain future following the loss of Nagorno-Karabakh, with citizens and officials grappling with the aftermath of the territorial loss and regional realignment.

item.title: Over 70 Drones Downed Near Moscow, Russian Officials Claim
item.description: Russian officials claimed over 70 Ukrainian drones were intercepted near Moscow in one of the largest aerial attacks on the Russian capital during the war.

item.title: MQ-9 Reaper Fleet Down to 135 Aircraft After 24 Shot Down Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military's MQ-9 Reaper drone fleet has been significantly depleted by combat losses during the Iran conflict, with two dozen aircraft destroyed over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pakistan Rejects Indian Claims on Cross-Border Operations
item.description: Pakistan's military and government rejected Indian accusations of cross-border infiltration, amid ongoing tensions between the nuclear-armed neighbors.

item.title: Trump Returns From China With Stability and Stalemate
item.description: The visit produced modest results by the standards of U.S.-China summits but highlighted a clear benefit for China: after the extremes of last year's trade war, the countries have reverted to their familiar economic and strategic standoff.

item.title: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Has Hit a Wall With Iran
item.description: Analysis of how President Trump's approach to Iran has reached an impasse, with limited diplomatic options as the conflict continues.

item.title: US Accuses Iraqi Man of Helping Iran-Backed Militias Plot Attacks on US and Europe
item.description: US authorities charged an Iraqi national with facilitating operations for Iran-backed militia groups planning attacks against American and European targets.

item.title: Federal Funding for Trump's Ballroom in Jeopardy After Senate Ruling
item.description: A U.S. Senate official removed security funding that could be used for President Trump's planned White House ballroom from a spending package, Democratic lawmakers said.

item.title: Far-Right Unite the Kingdom Rally in London
item.description: Live coverage of far-right demonstrations in London coinciding with Nakba Day commemorations, with security concerns around public order.

item.title: Secretary of State Approves $258 Billion in Weapons Sales to Middle East Allies
item.description: The US Secretary of State has approved massive arms sales to Middle Eastern allies amid ongoing regional conflicts and strategic partnerships.

item.title: RAF's New Kill System Blast
item.description: The Royal Air Force has deployed a new weapons system with enhanced capabilities for air operations.
